name: Upside Down Pear Gingerbread
recipeIngredient: 3 Ripe Pears, peeled, cored and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Lightly spray a 9-inch round or square cake pan.
3. Set aside.
4. In a large heavy skillet, heat 1/2 cup of butter and add 1/2 cup of brown sugar. Stir frequently until the sugar melts.
5. Add the pear slices and cook, stirring frequently, until the pears begin to soften. Pour into the prepared pan and set aside.
6. In a large bowl, beat together the remaining butter and brown sugar until light (about 1 minute).
7. Add the egg and molasses and continue to beat until well blended.
8. On a piece of wax paper, sift together the flour, ground ginger, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, baking soda, and salt.
9. Gently fold the dry ingredients into the butter mixture, alternating with 1 cup of boiling water. Do not overmix.
10. Spoon the batter over the prepared pears.
11. Bake in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes or until the cake is springy to the touch.
12. Remove the cake from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes.
13. Invert the cake onto a serving plate.
14. Slice into squares or wedges and serve with whipped cream if desired.
